text.title: Triage Protocol Failure Modes
text.summary: Five failure modes that erode the triage protocol. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Panic-patch — a sev-3 finding triggers an immediate code change that breaks an adjacent flow.
failure-mode.detector: SAME-DAY-FIX ticket closed in < 1 hour AND no QA pass logged AND a follow-up bug arrives within 48h
failure-mode.repair: Even SAME-DAY-FIX gets a 10-minute QA pass on adjacent flows. Add the QA-pass requirement to the action matrix. A real same-day fix takes 2-4 hours including verification.

failure-mode.description: Loud-user bias — one expressive user's frustration drives a SAME-DAY-FIX for a SINGLE-frequency issue.
failure-mode.detector: SAME-DAY-FIX action AND frequency_band = SINGLE AND no context-modifier present
failure-mode.repair: Re-evaluate per the matrix. SINGLE + no modifier = NEXT-SPRINT at most. Vivid user observations are data, not severity escalators.

failure-mode.description: Low-sev pile — every sev-1/sev-2 finding goes to BACKLOG and never returns. Quality erosion is invisible.
failure-mode.detector: BACKLOG has > 30 sev-1/sev-2 findings older than 6 months AND no batch-fix sprint has been scheduled
failure-mode.repair: Schedule a quarterly "low-sev fix" sprint OR add a rule: sev-1/2 with frequency MANY/MOST escalates to NEXT-SPRINT regardless. Low severity at high frequency = compounding pain.

failure-mode.description: Triage skipped because session was "just informal."
failure-mode.detector: Session notes exist but no triage-table-session-NNN.md was created
failure-mode.repair: Run the 30-minute triage. Informal sessions still produce findings that affect users. Skipping triage is how "we knew about that" becomes a postmortem line.

failure-mode.description: Context modifier abused — designer marks everything REVENUE-CRITICAL to push their pet finding to the top.
failure-mode.detector: > 60% of findings in a session marked REVENUE-CRITICAL OR the marked path is not actually a revenue path
failure-mode.repair: Define REVENUE-CRITICAL paths explicitly per product (checkout, signup, activation). Modifier requires path membership, not just claim. Audit triage tables periodically.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: If an LLM helps draft the triage table from session transcripts, it must not auto-escalate severity based on user emotional language ("frustrated", "angry") — those are inputs to the rubric, not direct severity outputs. The designer applies the rubric.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: The designer (or product owner) is the final triage decision-maker. Matrix output is a recommendation; overrides are written and reviewed. SAME-DAY-FIX actions are also surfaced to the engineering lead before commit, since they consume engineering capacity.
